triggerRules.name: Bad Day Containment
triggerRules.solution: Do not change pricing mid-week. Log the cause and continue execution.
triggerRules.situation: Any single day profit < 50% of daily target
triggerRules.reflection: What made this day weak? Identify the dominant cost driver.

triggerRules.name: Good Day Investigation
triggerRules.review: Flag that job as reference. Compare future jobs against it.
triggerRules.solution: Use this job as a benchmark for pricing, scope size, travel radius, and customer type.
triggerRules.situation: Any day profit ≥ 150–200% of daily target
triggerRules.reflection: What made this job structurally strong?

triggerRules.name: Travel Friction Alert
triggerRules.review: Should this job have been priced higher or bundled differently?
triggerRules.solution: Bundle similar jobs geographically or add a trip minimum charge.
triggerRules.situation: Mileage cost exceeds 15% of quoted price
triggerRules.reflection: Adjust future pricing rules for distance, add trip minimums.

triggerRules.name: Service Job Overreach
triggerRules.review: Are service jobs supporting overhead or consuming capacity?
triggerRules.solution: Raise minimum service pricing, enforce scope boundaries, or shift to project leads.
triggerRules.condition: 3+ service jobs in a row below target
triggerRules.situation: 3+ service jobs in a row below target profit
triggerRules.reflection: Pause accepting similar service jobs for 48 hours.

triggerRules.name: False Comfort Trigger
triggerRules.review: Is weekly performance dependent on a small number of outsized wins?
triggerRules.solution: Increase consistency by improving average job quality.
triggerRules.reflection: Mark the week as fragile. Do nothing operationally.

triggerRules.name: Below-Minimum Profit Risk
triggerRules.review: Is this job capable of contributing meaningfully to daily profit?
triggerRules.solution: Decline, reprice, or expand scope before acceptance.
triggerRules.situation: Estimated profit < minimum acceptable daily fraction
triggerRules.reflection: Can this job mathematically meet the minimum profit floor?

triggerRules.name: High Travel Friction
triggerRules.review: Does this job rely too heavily on pass-through material with limited margin?
triggerRules.solution: Increase markup, charge procurement fee, or decline low-margin material-heavy work.
triggerRules.situation: Material cost is a high percentage of quoted price
triggerRules.reflection: What is the actual labor margin after material pass-through?

triggerRules.name: Customer Risk Indicator
triggerRules.review: Does this customer increase execution or payment risk?
triggerRules.solution: Tighten terms, require deposit, or decline before engagement.
triggerRules.situation: Price resistance, scope creep signals, or urgency without clarity

phases.body:
Install one expansion coupling at mid-run or building entry for PVC runs over 30 ft. Desert ground temps in Palm Desert swing significantly. PVC expands ~4 in per 100 ft per 50°F temperature change. | [CEC 2022] 352.44 — PVC expansion fittings required where thermal expansion is a factor | Inspector may not fail for missing coupling but it prevents conduit cracking over time. | Desert climate — best practice for longevity. | 🟢 LOW | Material: expansion coupling ~$33 at markup
Labor: included in conduit run | Add to material order
phases.title: 75 ft PVC run in desert climate subject to significant thermal expansion.

phases.body:
At every metal j-box: connect EGC to box with listed grounding screw or clip. Connect all device ground screws. EMT with tight, listed fittings qualifies as EGC path — verify all connectors are listed and properly secured. | [CEC 2022] 250.148 — EGC at junction boxes
[CEC 2022] 250.118 — Listed metallic conduit as EGC | Inspector will spot-check j-boxes at rough-in. Missing EGC bonds are a common callout. | EMT with tight listed fittings qualifies — verify all connectors. | 🟡 MEDIUM | Labor: included in rough-in
Material: grounding screws ~$0.50/box — negligible | Standard practice — verify at rough-in
phases.title: All metal j-boxes throughout salon require EGC connection.

phases.body:
Conduit fill: 1 conductor = 53% max; 2 = 31% max; 3+ = 40% max. Support within 3 ft of every box and every 10 ft. Reamed and bushed at all terminations. Calculate fill before ordering conduit sizes — document calcs on plan or field sketch. | [CEC 2022] Ch.9 Table 1 — Fill %
[CEC 2022] Ch.9 Tables 4 & 5 — EMT + conductor dims
[CEC 2022] 358.30 — Support requirements | Inspector will check fill on home-run conduits. Over-filled conduit = correction notice. | Have fill calculations available for inspector. | 🟡 MEDIUM | Prevention: 30 min calculation
Remediation if wrong: 4–8 hrs re-pull = $380–$760 labor | Calculate before ordering conduit
phases.title: EMT is primary raceway throughout salon. All conduit fill, support, and termination rules apply.

phases.body:
Install TORK #2101 or approved equal photocell on roof. Run 1/2"C with pull string (minimum 12 ft per one-line detail) from photocell to time clock/contactor assembly. Photocell must be wet-location rated for rooftop installation. Mount where it has unobstructed sky view — not under HVAC equipment or awning shadow. | [CEC 2022] 410.136 — Wet-location rated devices
[CEC 2022] 225.22 — Raintight rooftop conduit
[E300 One-Line] — TORK #2101, 1/2"C w/pull string
[Title 24 Part 6] 140.9(a) — Outdoor automatic shutoff | Inspector will verify wet-location rating and raintight conduit at roof. Pull string is a plan requirement — install it. | Mount photocell away from RTU exhaust or HVAC airflow to prevent false triggering. | 🟡 MEDIUM | Labor: 1.5 hrs rooftop photocell install @ $95 = $143
Material: TORK 2101 ~$45 at markup + 1/2" conduit + pull string ~$20
Total: ~$208 | Confirm mounting location clears HVAC exhaust
phases.title: E300 One-Line: Photocell on roof, TORK #2101 or equal. Controls contactor coil (with time clock) for sign and controlled circuits. 1/2" conduit with pull string from photocell to time clock location.

phases.body:
Use RMC or IMC for all rooftop conduit runs (not exposed PVC). All fittings must be raintight. Stub-ups through roof: use LB fitting or weatherhead at penetration with approved roofing sealant. Apply temperature derating: rooftop ambient >104°F → use 90°C column (THWN-2) with 75°C termination value. Confirm all rooftop conductors are THWN-2 rated. | [CEC 2022] 225.22 — Raintight raceways on exterior surfaces
[CEC 2022] 310.15(B)(2)(a) — Temperature correction for hot environments
[CEC 2022] 300.5(D)(1) — Conduit stub-up protection | PVC on rooftop = immediate callout in Palm Desert. Inspector will check raintight fittings and roof penetration sealing. | Desert summer rooftop temps can hit 130°F+. THWN-2 is required — not optional. | 🟡 MEDIUM | Labor: included in RTU + photocell scope
Material: RMC fittings, LB, weatherhead, sealant ~$98 at markup | Spec THWN-2 on all rooftop circuits in wire order
phases.title: All rooftop conduit must be raintight. Palm Desert rooftop conduit in direct sun can exceed 130°F ambient in summer — THWN-2 rated conductors required.

phases.body:
Outlet boxes must be listed for luminaire weight: standard boxes rated 35 lbs max. Order chandelier box (listed for weight) if C exceeds 35 lbs. Pendant boxes must be at minimum listed for luminaire support. Verify rough-in box type matches fixture weight before ceiling is closed. Coordinate exact locations with architectural reflected ceiling plan. | [CEC 2022] 410.36 — Luminaire support, box weight rating
[CEC 2022] 314.27(A) — Outlet box for luminaire support | Inspector will check box type and listing for chandelier. Standard blue plastic boxes not listed for luminaire support. | Get fixture weights from owner spec sheet before rough-in. | 🟡 MEDIUM | Material: listed chandelier box ~$18–$45 if >35 lbs vs standard box ~$3
Labor: included in rough-in | Confirm fixture weight before ordering boxes
phases.title: E100: C = 50W decorative chandelier (1 unit), P = 20W decorative pendants (4 units). Owner-selected, contractor-provided.

phases.body:

Coordinate conduit routing with millwork contractor per E200 Power Note 6. ADA counter height requirements apply to the POS area. | [CBC 2022] Chapter 11B — Accessibility
[CEC 2022] 210.52 — Receptacle placement
[E200 Power Note 6] — Coordinate with owner + millwork
[AA-01] — Accessible POS location | Building inspector will verify accessible route to POS is clear of electrical conduit, covers, or boxes. | Coordinate POS outlet location with owner and millwork contractor before rough-in. | 🟢 LOW | Labor: included in receptacle rough-in
Coordination: 30 min with millwork contractor | Confirm POS location with owner and AA-01 dimensions

phases.body:

phases.body: 🔴 HIGH | Field measure clearance before framing. Get in writing with GC and salon owner. On-plan. | Re-location of panel post-rough: $1,500–$3,000+
phases.title: Panel is mounting in a 104 sq ft treatment room with salon equipment. Working clearance (3 ft × 30 in × 6.5 ft) must be field-verified before framing is locked in. Laser machine and treatment table cannot be in clearance zone.

phases.body: 🔴 HIGH | Add #6 AWG green THWN explicitly to wire order. Do not default to #10. | Material: 75 ft #6 AWG ~$57 at markup
phases.title: EGC for 150A feeder must be #6 AWG copper. Commonly ordered as #10 AWG (correct for 30A circuit, wrong here). Confirmed on E300 one-line as 1#6 GND.

phases.body:
Provide 72-hour demand load reading on the building service to the Engineer of Record (David Day P.E. / EDS Engineering – Jonathan Ortiz, Jonathan@edseng.org, 480-761-5994) PRIOR to start of any work. In lieu of 72hr reading, owner may request 12-month peak demand from SCE. NO WORK SHALL COMMENCE until engineer receives one of these. Include cost in bid. | [E300 Load Test Note] — Contractual engineering requirement on permit documents | If Palm Desert inspector asks for this at rough-in and you don't have it, expect stop-work. Document with a written report to the engineer. | Coordinate with owner to request SCE 12-month peak demand as the faster alternative. | 🔴 HIGH | Demand logger rental: ~$150–$300 OR SCE data request (free via owner)
Coordination: 1 hr @ $95 = $95
⚠️ SCOPE GAP — must be in bid | Do before mobilizing
phases.title: [Rough-In] E300 Load Test Note requires demand reading BEFORE any work starts.

phases.body: Any field condition that requires a deviation from the approved one-line diagram or panel schedule must be RFI'd to EDS Engineering before proceeding. This includes conduit routing changes, conductor size deviations, or equipment substitutions. Document all RFIs in writing. | [E300 One-Line Note B] — Design change control requirement | Inspector will reject work that deviates from approved drawings without engineer approval on record. | Palm Desert Clariti portal — upload RFI responses to permit file if available. | 🔴 HIGH | Admin — no direct cost. Risk of non-compliance: re-inspection + remediation | Establish RFI process with engineer before mobilizing
phases.title: E300 One-Line Note B states no design changes without prior approval of design engineer AND electrical inspector.

phases.body:
Install new Panel PA: 200A bus, MLO (Main Lug Only — fed from SES via 150A/2P breaker), 240/120V 2P 3W, NEMA 1 Flush, AIC rating ≥ 22,000. Remove FPE Stab-Lok completely. 42-space minimum per scope. Mount in Treatment Room 104 per panel schedule. Confirm 3 ft × 30 in × 6.5 ft working clearance in Treatment Room 104 — it is only 104 sq ft with salon equipment. | [CEC 2022] 110.9 — AIC rating ≥ available fault current
[CEC 2022] 408.36 — Overcurrent protection ≤ bus rating
[CEC 2022] 110.26(A) — Working clearance
[E300] Fault Schedule: PA fault = 12,033A, AIC = 22,000 | Inspector WILL open panel. AIC label must show ≥ 22,000. FPE left in place = automatic correction. Panel spec must match approved drawings exactly. | Palm Desert: Electric final required before SCE meter release. Panel room = Treatment Room 104 — confirm no equipment in 3 ft clearance zone. | 🔴 HIGH | Labor: 8–10 hrs @ $95 = $760–$950
Material: 200A/42-sp panel 22kAIC ~$450–$600 (at 150% markup)
Total: ~$1,210–$1,550
⚠️ Do NOT order standard 10kAIC panel | Confirm AIC rating before ordering
phases.title: [Finish] New Panel 'PA' per approved drawings. Panel AIC must be minimum 22,000 — fault current at PA calculated at 12,033A. Standard 10,000 AIC residential panels are NOT acceptable.

phases.body:
All circuit breaker positions must be labeled on typed directory inside panel door per NEC 408.4(A). Additionally, permanently mark (permanent marker) circuit number on coverplate of every receptacle, equipment connection, and lighting j-box. This is an EDS Engineering project requirement beyond basic NEC — it will be checked. | [CEC 2022] 408.4(A) — Circuit directory required
[E300 Panel Schedule Note C] — Permanent marker on all devices/boxes | Inspector will open panel AND spot-check device coverplates. Stick-on labels will fail this requirement. | Build directory labeling and device marking into final inspection prep. Allow 2 hrs for 42-circuit panel. | 🟡 MEDIUM | Labor: 2 hrs marking + directory @ $95 = $190
Material: permanent markers, label tape ~$8 | Pre-final task — schedule day before inspection
phases.title: [Trim] Panel schedule note C from E300: Circuit IDs must be permanently marked with permanent marker on coverplates of receptacles, equipment, and lighting j-boxes. Stick-on labels NOT acceptable.

phases.body:
Any MWBC in Panel PA must use a 2-pole breaker or approved handle tie. Pigtail all neutrals at every device — never rely on device screw for neutral continuity. Identify all MWBCs on panel schedule. In this 120/240V 2P system, MWBC hots must be on opposite legs (L1 and L2) to cancel on neutral. | [CEC 2022] 210.4(B) — Simultaneous disconnect for MWBC
[CEC 2022] 300.13(B) — Neutral continuity
[E300 Panel Schedule Note D] | Single-pole breakers on MWBC = automatic fail. Inspector will check at panel and at device locations. | Flag all MWBCs on panel schedule before permit final. Confirm handle ties installed at panel. | 🔴 HIGH | Labor: 0.5 hr/MWBC location for pigtailing @ $95 = ~$48/location
Handle ties ~$3/pair | Flag on panel schedule before rough-in
phases.title: [Rough-In] E300 Panel Schedule Note D: All multiwire branch circuits (MWBCs) must have a device that disconnects ALL ungrounded conductors simultaneously at point of origin.

phases.body:
Pull 3 × 1/0 AWG THWN-2 copper + 1 × #6 AWG green EGC in 2" conduit, end-to-end, no underground splices. Ampacity: 1/0 AWG = 150A @ 75°C ✅. Voltage drop at 75 ft = 1.15% ✅. Conduit fill: 3 CCCs = no derating required ✅. Replace existing 90A/2P breaker at SES with new 150A/2P — confirm SES has available space and busing for 150A/2P. NOTE: Drawings show feeder length as 65 ft (for calc only) — your field condition is 75 ft, which is acceptable per E300 Note C. | [CEC 2022] 310.15(B) — 1/0 AWG = 150A @ 75°C
[CEC 2022] 310.15(B)(3)(a) — No derating at 3 CCCs
[CEC 2022] 250.122 — #6 AWG EGC for 150A OCPD
[E300 One-Line] — 3#1/0, 1#6 GND, 2"C confirmed
[E300 Note 1] — Replace 90A/2P with 150A/2P at SES | Inspector will verify conductor size, EGC size, and conduit fill at both termination points. 65 ft shown on drawings — your 75 ft field length is noted in as-built. | Palm Desert: Underground service required. Underground inspection via Clariti BEFORE backfill. | 🔴 HIGH | Labor: 6 hrs pull + terminate @ $95 = $570
Material: 1/0 AWG THWN-2 ×3 × 75 ft + #6 GND × 75 ft ~$437 at markup
Total feeder wire: ~$1,007
⚠️ 2" conduit — verify stub-up and panel knockout match | Confirm SES space for 150A/2P before ordering breaker
phases.title: [Underground] Approved one-line (E300) specifies: 3#1/0, 1#6 GND, 2"C for feeder from SES to new Panel PA. Replace existing 90A/2P breaker in SES with new 150A/2P breaker.

phases.body:
STEP 1: GPR slab scan REQUIRED before saw-cut. El Paseo commercial era — high PT slab risk.
STEP 2: Saw-cut and break concrete.
STEP 3: Intercept conduit. Pull new 1/0 AWG end-to-end — no underground splice.
STEP 4: Install new stub-up aligned with panel knockout. RMC/IMC from below slab. Bushing at top.
STEP 5: Seal penetration. Firestop if elevated slab.
STEP 6: Concrete patch (sub trade).
Confirm concrete work covered on permit. | [CBC 2022] 1801/1901 — Slab alteration / PT slab risk
[CEC 2022] 300.5(D)(1) — Stub-up protection + bushing
[CEC 2022] 300.5(E) — No underground splices
[E200] — 'SAW, CUT, & PATCH AS REQUIRED' shown on drawings | GPR scan results must be on site. No scan = stop work if slab is PT. Stub-up must align with panel knockout before patch pour. | ⚠️ MAJOR CHANGE ORDER RISK — GPR, concrete sub, and patch frequently not in electrical scope. Assign in writing to GC. | 🔴 HIGH | GPR scan: $350–$600
Saw-cut + break: $380–$800
Conduit intercept + stub: Labor 6 hrs = $570; Material ~$120
Concrete patch (sub): $400–$800
TOTAL: ~$1,700–$2,890 ⚠️ SCOPE GAP | Assign GPR and patch to GC in writing
phases.title: [Underground] E200 Power Floor Plan shows 'SAW, CUT, & PATCH AS REQUIRED' at panel location. Wall moving 2 ft forward requires conduit intercept, re-route, and new stub-up.

phases.body:
Pull #6 AWG green THWN-2 EGC with feeder conductors in 2" conduit. Bond to SES grounding electrode system and to new Panel PA ground bus. Existing grounding system to remain per E300 one-line note. | [CEC 2022] 250.122 — Table: 150A OCPD → #6 AWG EGC minimum
[CEC 2022] 250.119 — Green or bare identification
[E300 One-Line] — 1#6 GND confirmed in feeder callout | Inspector will check EGC size. Commonly undersized to #10 AWG — incorrect for 150A. #6 minimum. | Confirm EGC shown on permit one-line diagram. | 🔴 HIGH | Material: #6 AWG green × 75 ft ~$57 at markup
⚠️ Often missing from wire order — add explicitly | Add to wire schedule
phases.title: EGC for 150A overcurrent device must be #6 AWG copper. Confirmed on E300 one-line: 1#6 GND.

phases.body:
At every metal j-box: connect EGC to box with listed grounding screw or clip. Connect all device ground screws. EMT with tight, listed fittings qualifies as EGC path — verify all connectors are listed and properly secured. | [CEC 2022] 250.148 — EGC at junction boxes
[CEC 2022] 250.118 — Listed metallic conduit as EGC | Inspector will spot-check j-boxes at rough-in. Missing EGC bonds are a common callout. | EMT with tight listed fittings qualifies — verify all connectors. | 🟡 MEDIUM | Labor: included in rough-in
Material: grounding screws ~$0.50/box — negligible | Standard practice — verify at rough-in
phases.title: [Rough-In] All metal j-boxes throughout salon require EGC connection.

phases.body:
Pigtail neutral at every outlet/switch on all MWBCs. Never rely on device screw for neutral continuity. Tripping one MWBC breaker must not interrupt neutral for other circuits. Coordinate with panel schedule to identify all MWBC circuits. | [CEC 2022] 300.13(B) — Neutral continuity on MWBCs
[E300 Panel Schedule Note D] — Simultaneous disconnect required | Inspector will check. Unpigtailed MWBC neutral = shock hazard + failed inspection. | Identify all MWBCs on panel schedule before rough-in. | 🔴 HIGH | Labor: 0.25 hr extra per MWBC device for pigtailing @ $95 = ~$24/location | Flag all MWBCs on panel schedule
phases.title: [Rough-In] Any MWBCs in the salon: neutral must be pigtailed at every device, never device-dependent.

PA-3 (Laser E1): 3/4"C, 2#12, #12G

EF-1 is interlocked with the nLight lighting control device — when lights turn on, EF-1 turns on; when lights turn off, EF-1 turns off. Turn lights off → EF-1 must de-energize. | ⚠️ Control wire run from lighting switch/sensor to EF-1 is easy to miss at rough-in. Plan conduit stub for this during rough-in. | 🟡 MEDIUM | Labor: 1 hr control wire run + interlock wiring @ $95 = $95

── PRE-CONSTRUCTION REQUIREMENTS (FROM APPROVED DRAWINGS) ──
★ 72-Hr Demand Load Reading
E300 Load Test Note requires demand reading BEFORE any work starts.

Provide 72-hour demand load reading on the building service to the Engineer of Record (David Day P.E. / EDS Engineering – Jonathan Ortiz, Jonathan@edseng.org, 480-761-5994) PRIOR to start of any work. In lieu of 72hr reading, owner may request 12-month peak demand from SCE. NO WORK SHALL COMMENCE until engineer receives one of these. Include cost in bid.
[E300 Load Test Note] — Contractual engineering requirement on permit documents
If Palm Desert inspector asks for this at rough-in and you don't have it, expect stop-work. Document with a written report to the engineer.
Coordinate with owner to request SCE 12-month peak demand as the faster alternative.
🔴 HIGH

Demand logger rental: ~$150–$300 OR SCE data request (free via owner)
Coordination: 1 hr @ $95 = $95
⚠️ SCOPE GAP — must be in bid
Do before mobilizing
★ No Design Changes Without Engineer Approval
E300 One-Line Note B states no design changes without prior approval of design engineer AND electrical inspector.

Any field condition that requires a deviation from the approved one-line diagram or panel schedule must be RFI'd to EDS Engineering before proceeding. This includes conduit routing changes, conductor size deviations, or equipment substitutions. Document all RFIs in writing.
[E300 One-Line Note B] — Design change control requirement
Inspector will reject work that deviates from approved drawings without engineer approval on record.
Palm Desert Clariti portal — upload RFI responses to permit file if available.
🔴 HIGH
Admin — no direct cost. Risk of non-compliance: re-inspection + remediation
Establish RFI process with engineer before mobilizing
── ELECTRICAL DISTRIBUTION SYSTEM ──
★ Sub-Panel PA — 200A MLO, AIC 22,000 Minimum
New Panel 'PA' per approved drawings. Panel AIC must be minimum 22,000 — fault current at PA calculated at 12,033A. Standard 10,000 AIC residential panels are NOT acceptable.

Install new Panel PA: 200A bus, MLO (Main Lug Only — fed from SES via 150A/2P breaker), 240/120V 2P 3W, NEMA 1 Flush, AIC rating ≥ 22,000. Remove FPE Stab-Lok completely. 42-space minimum per scope. Mount in Treatment Room 104 per panel schedule. Confirm 3 ft × 30 in × 6.5 ft working clearance in Treatment Room 104 — it is only 104 sq ft with salon equipment.

[CEC 2022] 110.9 — AIC rating ≥ available fault current
[CEC 2022] 408.36 — Overcurrent protection ≤ bus rating
[CEC 2022] 110.26(A) — Working clearance
[E300] Fault Schedule: PA fault = 12,033A, AIC = 22,000
Inspector WILL open panel. AIC label must show ≥ 22,000. FPE left in place = automatic correction. Panel spec must match approved drawings exactly.
Palm Desert: Electric final required before SCE meter release. Panel room = Treatment Room 104 — confirm no equipment in 3 ft clearance zone.
🔴 HIGH

Labor: 8–10 hrs @ $95 = $760–$950
Material: 200A/42-sp panel 22kAIC ~$450–$600 (at 150% markup)
Total: ~$1,210–$1,550
⚠️ Do NOT order standard 10kAIC panel
Confirm AIC rating before ordering
★ Panel Schedule Circuit Directory — Permanent Marker Required
Panel schedule note C from E300: Circuit IDs must be permanently marked with permanent marker on coverplates of receptacles, equipment, and lighting j-boxes. Stick-on labels NOT acceptable.

All circuit breaker positions must be labeled on typed directory inside panel door per NEC 408.4(A). Additionally, permanently mark (permanent marker) circuit number on coverplate of every receptacle, equipment connection, and lighting j-box. This is an EDS Engineering project requirement beyond basic NEC — it will be checked.

[CEC 2022] 408.4(A) — Circuit directory required
[E300 Panel Schedule Note C] — Permanent marker on all devices/boxes
Inspector will open panel AND spot-check device coverplates. Stick-on labels will fail this requirement.
Build directory labeling and device marking into final inspection prep. Allow 2 hrs for 42-circuit panel.
🟡 MEDIUM

Labor: 2 hrs marking + directory @ $95 = $190
Material: permanent markers, label tape ~$8
Pre-final task — schedule day before inspection
★ MWBC — Simultaneous Disconnect Required
E300 Panel Schedule Note D: All multiwire branch circuits (MWBCs) must have a device that disconnects ALL ungrounded conductors simultaneously at point of origin.

Any MWBC in Panel PA must use a 2-pole breaker or approved handle tie. Pigtail all neutrals at every device — never rely on device screw for neutral continuity. Identify all MWBCs on panel schedule. In this 120/240V 2P system, MWBC hots must be on opposite legs (L1 and L2) to cancel on neutral.

[CEC 2022] 210.4(B) — Simultaneous disconnect for MWBC
[CEC 2022] 300.13(B) — Neutral continuity
[E300 Panel Schedule Note D]
Single-pole breakers on MWBC = automatic fail. Inspector will check at panel and at device locations.
Flag all MWBCs on panel schedule before permit final. Confirm handle ties installed at panel.
🔴 HIGH

Labor: 0.5 hr/MWBC location for pigtailing @ $95 = ~$48/location
Handle ties ~$3/pair
Flag on panel schedule before rough-in
── FEEDER & UNDERGROUND SYSTEM ──
★ Feeder Conductors — 3×1/0 AWG + #6 GND in 2" Conduit
Approved one-line (E300) specifies: 3#1/0, 1#6 GND, 2"C for feeder from SES to new Panel PA. Replace existing 90A/2P breaker in SES with new 150A/2P breaker.

Pull 3 × 1/0 AWG THWN-2 copper + 1 × #6 AWG green EGC in 2" conduit, end-to-end, no underground splices. Ampacity: 1/0 AWG = 150A @ 75°C ✅. Voltage drop at 75 ft = 1.15% ✅. Conduit fill: 3 CCCs = no derating required ✅. Replace existing 90A/2P breaker at SES with new 150A/2P — confirm SES has available space and busing for 150A/2P. NOTE: Drawings show feeder length as 65 ft (for calc only) — your field condition is 75 ft, which is acceptable per E300 Note C.

[CEC 2022] 310.15(B) — 1/0 AWG = 150A @ 75°C
[CEC 2022] 310.15(B)(3)(a) — No derating at 3 CCCs
[CEC 2022] 250.122 — #6 AWG EGC for 150A OCPD
[E300 One-Line] — 3#1/0, 1#6 GND, 2"C confirmed
[E300 Note 1] — Replace 90A/2P with 150A/2P at SES
Inspector will verify conductor size, EGC size, and conduit fill at both termination points. 65 ft shown on drawings — your 75 ft field length is noted in as-built.
Palm Desert: Underground service required. Underground inspection via Clariti BEFORE backfill.
🔴 HIGH

Labor: 6 hrs pull + terminate @ $95 = $570
Material: 1/0 AWG THWN-2 ×3 × 75 ft + #6 GND × 75 ft ~$437 at markup
Total feeder wire: ~$1,007
⚠️ 2" conduit — verify stub-up and panel knockout match
Confirm SES space for 150A/2P before ordering breaker
★ Concrete Break + GPR Scan + Stub-Up Relocation
E200 Power Floor Plan shows 'SAW, CUT, & PATCH AS REQUIRED' at panel location. Wall moving 2 ft forward requires conduit intercept, re-route, and new stub-up.

STEP 1: GPR slab scan REQUIRED before saw-cut. El Paseo commercial era — high PT slab risk.
STEP 2: Saw-cut and break concrete.
STEP 3: Intercept conduit. Pull new 1/0 AWG end-to-end — no underground splice.
STEP 4: Install new stub-up aligned with panel knockout. RMC/IMC from below slab. Bushing at top.
STEP 5: Seal penetration. Firestop if elevated slab.
STEP 6: Concrete patch (sub trade).
Confirm concrete work covered on permit.

[CBC 2022] 1801/1901 — Slab alteration / PT slab risk
[CEC 2022] 300.5(D)(1) — Stub-up protection + bushing
[CEC 2022] 300.5(E) — No underground splices
[E200] — 'SAW, CUT, & PATCH AS REQUIRED' shown on drawings
GPR scan results must be on site. No scan = stop work if slab is PT. Stub-up must align with panel knockout before patch pour.
⚠️ MAJOR CHANGE ORDER RISK — GPR, concrete sub, and patch frequently not in electrical scope. Assign in writing to GC.
🔴 HIGH

GPR scan: $350–$600
Saw-cut + break: $380–$800
Conduit intercept + stub: Labor 6 hrs = $570; Material ~$120
Concrete patch (sub): $400–$800
TOTAL: ~$1,700–$2,890 ⚠️ SCOPE GAP
Assign GPR and patch to GC in writing
PVC Expansion Coupling — 75 ft Feeder Run
75 ft PVC run in desert climate subject to significant thermal expansion.
Install one expansion coupling at mid-run or building entry for PVC runs over 30 ft. Desert ground temps in Palm Desert swing significantly. PVC expands ~4 in per 100 ft per 50°F temperature change.
[CEC 2022] 352.44 — PVC expansion fittings required where thermal expansion is a factor
Inspector may not fail for missing coupling but it prevents conduit cracking over time.
Desert climate — best practice for longevity.
🟢 LOW

Material: expansion coupling ~$33 at markup
Labor: included in conduit run
Add to material order
── GROUNDING & BONDING ──
★ EGC at 150A Feeder — #6 AWG Minimum
EGC for 150A overcurrent device must be #6 AWG copper. Confirmed on E300 one-line: 1#6 GND.
Pull #6 AWG green THWN-2 EGC with feeder conductors in 2" conduit. Bond to SES grounding electrode system and to new Panel PA ground bus. Existing grounding system to remain per E300 one-line note.

EGC at Every Junction Box — Bond to Metal Boxes
All metal j-boxes throughout salon require EGC connection.

At every metal j-box: connect EGC to box with listed grounding screw or clip. Connect all device ground screws. EMT with tight, listed fittings qualifies as EGC path — verify all connectors are listed and properly secured.

[CEC 2022] 250.148 — EGC at junction boxes
[CEC 2022] 250.118 — Listed metallic conduit as EGC
Inspector will spot-check j-boxes at rough-in. Missing EGC bonds are a common callout.
EMT with tight listed fittings qualifies — verify all connectors.

Labor: included in rough-in
Material: grounding screws ~$0.50/box — negligible
Standard practice — verify at rough-in
Neutral Continuity on MWBCs — Pigtail Required
Any MWBCs in the salon: neutral must be pigtailed at every device, never device-dependent.

Pigtail neutral at every outlet/switch on all MWBCs. Never rely on device screw for neutral continuity. Tripping one MWBC breaker must not interrupt neutral for other circuits. Coordinate with panel schedule to identify all MWBC circuits.

[CEC 2022] 300.13(B) — Neutral continuity on MWBCs
[E300 Panel Schedule Note D] — Simultaneous disconnect required
Inspector will check. Unpigtailed MWBC neutral = shock hazard + failed inspection.
Identify all MWBCs on panel schedule before rough-in.
🔴 HIGH
Labor: 0.25 hr extra per MWBC device for pigtailing @ $95 = ~$24/location
Flag all MWBCs on panel schedule

EMT Conduit — Primary Raceway, Fill and Support
EMT is primary raceway throughout salon. All conduit fill, support, and termination rules apply.

Conduit fill: 1 conductor = 53% max; 2 = 31% max; 3+ = 40% max. Support within 3 ft of every box and every 10 ft. Reamed and bushed at all terminations. Calculate fill before ordering conduit sizes — document calcs on plan or field sketch.

[CEC 2022] Ch.9 Table 1 — Fill %
[CEC 2022] Ch.9 Tables 4 & 5 — EMT + conductor dims
[CEC 2022] 358.30 — Support requirements
Inspector will check fill on home-run conduits. Over-filled conduit = correction notice.
Have fill calculations available for inspector.

Remediation if wrong: 4–8 hrs re-pull = $380–$760 labor
Calculate before ordering conduit
★ MC Cable — 12/2 Home Run Branches, Support Rules
MC cable used for branch home runs. Support and termination rules apply.

Support MC cable within 12 in of every box and every 6 ft throughout run. Use listed MC staples or straps. Terminate with listed MC connectors — no exposed armor inside boxes. Do NOT pull MC inside EMT (separate wiring methods — cannot combine).

[CEC 2022] 330.30 — MC cable support
[CEC 2022] 330.40 — Listed MC fittings required
MC support spacing is very common rough-in callout. Inspector will look for unsupported spans.

Shampoo bowl areas are wet locations — all equipment must be suitable for wet/damp locations. Hair Wash Station on dedicated circuit PA-2.

[CEC 2022] 210.8(B)(1) — GFCI within 6 ft of sink in commercial occupancy
[E200 Power Note B] — Confirmed on approved drawings

New Panel 'PA' per approved drawings. Panel AIC must be minimum 22,000 — fault current at PA calculated at 12,033A. Standard 10,000 AIC residential panels are NOT acceptable.

Install new Panel PA: 200A bus, MLO (Main Lug Only — fed from SES via 150A/2P breaker), 240/120V 2P 3W, NEMA 1 Flush, AIC rating ≥ 22,000. Remove FPE Stab-Lok completely. 42-space minimum per scope. E300 Panel Schedule Note D: All multiwire branch circuits (MWBCs) must have a device that disconnects ALL ungrounded conductors simultaneously at point of origin.

Any MWBC in Panel PA must use a 2-pole breaker or approved handle tie. Pigtail all neutrals at every device — never rely on device screw for neutral continuity. Identify all MWBCs on panel schedule. In this 120/240V 2P system, MWBC hots must be on opposite legs (L1 and L2) to cancel on neutral.

★ Feeder Conductors — 3×1/0 AWG + #6 GND in 2" Conduit
Approved one-line (E300) specifies: 3#1/0, 1#6 GND, 2"C for feeder from SES to new Panel PA. Replace existing 90A/2P breaker in SES with new 150A/2P breaker.

Pull 3 × 1/0 AWG THWN-2 copper + 1 × #6 AWG green EGC in 2" conduit, end-to-end, no underground splices. Ampacity: 1/0 AWG = 150A @ 75°C ✅. Voltage drop at 75 ft = 1.15% ✅. Conduit fill: 3 CCCs = no derating required ✅. Replace existing 90A/2P breaker at SES with new 150A/2P — confirm SES has available space and busing for 150A/2P. NOTE: Drawings show feeder length as 65 ft (for calc only) — your field condition is 75 ft, which is acceptable per E300 Note C.

★ Concrete Break + GPR Scan + Stub-Up Relocation
E200 Power Floor Plan shows 'SAW, CUT, & PATCH AS REQUIRED' at panel location. Wall moving 2 ft forward requires conduit intercept, re-route, and new stub-up.

STEP 1: GPR slab scan REQUIRED before saw-cut. El Paseo commercial era — high PT slab risk.
STEP 2: Saw-cut and break concrete.
